title: Perfection That Feels Unstable
description: Hino escapes a family gathering and meets a mysterious, beautiful woman in the garden. Later, Nagafuji arrives with a massive backpack—just as chaotic and familiar as always.
content:
Once I figured I'd stayed long enough, I seized the first excuse I could think of and escaped from the grown-ups' gathering. My parents know my temperament well enough by now, so they didn't complain. As for the other family—whether I was there or not made no difference to them anyway.

As long as I showed my face, my duty was done. My brothers handled everything else at home, which was a huge relief. I was exhausted.

I loosened the hair that had been neatly pinned up, and even the air around me seemed to relax.

The garden looked the same as it did every year, drowned in a deafening chorus of cicadas. Evening was already settling in, but they shrieked with the same enthusiasm as midday. It made me wonder if they'd divided themselves into shifts, taking turns to scream. Too many trees are more trouble than they're worth—maintaining them is a nightmare.

I stepped into the sticky heat of the yard, rolling my stiff shoulders and stretching my back. That was when I sensed movement nearby—someone's presence, someone's footsteps.

I turned around.

A woman wreathed in a soft floral scent was standing right beside me, grinning.

She was from the other family—specifically, the one who looked most impatient during the formal greetings earlier. I'd gotten the sense she hated those stiff social gatherings as much as I did. The only difference was that, unlike me, she hadn't dropped her polite smile even once. So she'd snuck out too, huh.

She stretched lightly, then began studying my face with curious enthusiasm.

We had a huge height difference. And… chest difference.

'What?' I muttered.

'High schooler?'

She asked it with a bright, easy smile—as though the question were of life-and-death importance.

…She was gorgeous. And she was the type of beauty who simply didn't exist in my usual surroundings.

'Yeah.'

'Mhm.'

What was with that look? Like she'd just stumbled on a hidden treasure.

'You're pretty cute. Where do you live?'

'Right here.'

'Aha~hahaha.'

She burst into a free, ringing laugh. Everything about her movements felt unrestrained yet somehow elegant. Nagafuji always says my home has that kind of atmosphere—I guess this must be what she meant.

The lively woman pulled a phone from her sleeve, blinked at the screen, then widened her eyes.

'Well, that's rare…'

When she tucked the phone away, her expression had softened into a cheerful, relieved smile—like something good had just happened.

'Good,' she mouthed silently.

Then she bowed.

'Well then, I'll take my leave.'

'Oh… sure.'

With that, she strode off lightly.

'…What was that about,' I muttered.

Beautiful, yes—yet suspicious. Her flawless, polished exterior made her harder to trust. Maybe that's why we instinctively grow wary of people who pretend to be perfect. Imperfection is the more natural shape of a human being.

'Young lady.'

A familiar voice called out behind me next.

'Don't call me that,' I groaned.

'I call you that precisely because you hate it.'

She gave a soft little laugh.

'You're awful. Anyway, what is it?'

'You have a visitor.'

'Huh? Oh—yeah, I get it. That girl never warns me she's coming.' And showing up after sunset? Obviously planning to stay the night. Typical.

Crossing the yard with the housekeeper, I spotted Nagafuji on the way to the gate.

She had a massive backpack on. The moment she saw me in my yukata, her glasses caught the light as she grinned.

'Ooh~ it's Hino in a kimono!'

'You're such a pain.'

'Hino with her hair down looks great too~'

'What's so great about it?'

Could she stop wandering around my garden like she owned the place?

'I saw someone else in a yukata just now,' she added.

'That's one of our guests.'

'She asked if I was a high schooler. I told her I'm the butcher's daughter, and she got super excited.'

'What kind of conversation is that supposed to be?'

I was honestly speechless. Thinking I was questioning her, Nagafuji puffed up and declared:

'It's me—your flawless, perfect Nagafuji~'

'Yeah, yeah. Sure.'

She was perfect from head to toe—yet somehow, never suspicious. If Nagafuji ever became a completely calm, stable person, that would be unsettling.

Thinking that, I finally let myself laugh.
